conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility rules. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

To start, do you have any children under the age of 16 (or under 20 if they're in approved education or training)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Do you live with Alex, or does Alex live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Just to confirm - is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or are there any other special circumstances regarding Alex's living situation that I should know about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in care for a couple of months, but it hasn't been too long.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. This is an important detail for determining your eligibility.

When you say Alex has been in care for a couple of months, can you tell me:

1. How many weeks exactly has Alex been in care? 
2. Does Alex spend any regular time at home with you each week (for example, 24 hours or more per week, or at least 2 nights in a row each week)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been 7 weeks exactly. And no, Alex doesn't come home regularly - they're at the care home full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One final question: During these 7 weeks that Alex has been in care, have you been regularly spending money on Alex - for example, on clothes, presents, food, pocket money, or other items for their upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have been spending money on Alex - buying clothes and things they need while they're there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news - you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!

Here's why:

**You meet the basic eligibility criteria:**
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for Alex
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Regarding Alex being in care:**
Even though Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, the rules allow you to continue receiving Child Benefit for **8 weeks** after a child goes into care (if nobody else claims). Since it's been 7 weeks, you're still within this period.

**Additionally:** Because you're regularly spending money on Alex's upkeep (buying clothes and things they need), you could potentially continue receiving Child Benefit for **longer than 8 weeks**. The rules state that if you contribute to your child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment, you can continue to receive it beyond the initial 8-week period.

**Important note:** You should contact the Child Benefit Office to inform them that Alex is in care, as this is a change in circumstances that needs to be reported. They can provide specific guidance about your ongoing eligibility, especially as you approach or pass the 8-week mark.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Based on the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Alex is 6 years old, so under 16. You live in the UK (Manchester). Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, the rules state that you can continue to receive Child Benefit for 8 weeks after your child goes into care if nobody else claims. Since it has been 7 weeks (which is still within the 8-week period) and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you remain eligible. Additionally, you are regularly spending money on Alex's upkeep (buying clothes and things they need), which can allow payments to continue beyond 8 weeks as contributions to upkeep. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with one child, Alex (age 6). Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is still within the 8-week period during which Child Benefit can continue. The claimant is also making regular financial contributions to Alex's upkeep, which could extend eligibility beyond 8 weeks if needed. No one else is claiming for Alex. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit.
